title: A world cleansed of original sin and the Fruits
display_title: A world cleansed of original sin and the Fruits
wikitext:
[]: "A world cleansed of original sin" and the Fruits             Quote Post by unitM » Sun Dec 22, 2013 2:22 am I noticed recently there has been some discussion around the concept of the biblical fruits used in Evangelion(the Fruit of Knowledge etc). I found this interesting quote from Gendou in 2.22. It plays when Fuyu and Gendou are observing an impact site, which is zoomed in one as Gendou speaks, from space: Gendou: However, there are people who wished for this disaster. A world cleansed of original sin that no man can enter.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Dec 22, 2013 3:40 pm It's just a metaphor, bro.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Sun Dec 22, 2013 3:48 pm I don't know if it is. It really isn't that far of a stretch from "original sin" to "AT fields" if you think about it: original sin comes at least in part from the differences in people, and Evangelion has drawn attention to this dialogue of differences plenty of times. I'm only referring to it as the AT field, of course, but it's not like the show has never approached the concept of differences in people. You take what I said and go back to explain one of Gendou's ambiguous quotes: Gendou: However, there are people who wished [Shinji] for this disaster [Instrumentality] . [Description of the disaster:] A world cleansed of original sin [Shinji running away from pain and penultimately concluding that he wanted the world to be free of pain] that no man can enter.

[]: Re: "A world cleansed of original sin" and the Fru             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 22, 2013 3:53 pm unitM wrote: Can the Fruit of Knowledge be the activation of the AT field in living organisms?

[]: Re: "A world cleansed of original sin" and the Fru             Quote Post by unitM » Sun Dec 22, 2013 3:58 pm Reichu wrote: Angels have A.T. Fields. In fact, in these new movies, it's the destabilization of the ATF upon death that causes them to explode into red goo. Kind of familiar, isn't it?

[]: Re: "A world cleansed of original sin" and the Fru             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 22, 2013 4:12 pm unitM : The only thing really "special" about Lilin's A.T. Fields is that they aren't capable of psychokinetic feats. Otherwise, in both Lilin and Angels, the ATF is the boundary between one individual and another. The Angels take this to especially ludicrous lengths, with their superhuman ability to individually differentiate, and to separate each other via physical barriers and not just mental ones. I honestly have no idea what "original sin" is supposed to be referring to, but these might be interesting reading. (Seach for phrase "original sin" to skip to the relevant parts.) http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/1493/Red-Earth-LCL-and-things-I-dont-understand/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6163/Question-on-Gods-existence-in-Evangelion/ http://forum.evageeks.org/post/449020/Angels-The-Child-Interpretation/ http://forum.evageeks.org/post/434296/Shinjis-portrayal-in-Eva-20-222/

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Dec 22, 2013 4:32 pm Gendo's comments make absolutely zero sense if you take "original sin" to mean what it means in the biblical tradition. The actual eating of the fruit is irrelevant. The "sin" in question is defiance of God's commandment. Gold told Adam and Eve what they could and could not eat and warned them specifically away from the Tree of the Knowledge of Good and Evil and the Tree of Life. Thus in that context what Seele did is resonant with Eve's sin in the Bible. They took forbidden knowledge, and were cast down for it. Hence the similarity of the fallout to biblical plagues, especially the waters turning to blood and general infertility which resembles the culling of the first born. (Note also that all of the pilots are first born children, and Misato's presence at the experiment that caused SI may have been as a literal sacrifice of a first born child) There's a repeated theme in the bible of various characters defying, trying to reach, mocking, or otherwise challenging God and his/their authority and facing severe punishment for it, including Moses himself. The area, then, is not "clean" of original sin, its barrenness is a direct consequence of it. Unless Gendo is using "original sin" as a kind of kenning , a poetic device to refer to all of humanity/life. An area that is uninhabited has no sin. Isn't he looking at the ship with the Lance on it as he says this? He's saying the area is cleansed of original sin at the same time he's overseeing it reenacted again. The Adam and Eve narrative has occult implications that completely change the context of Gendo's comment. In some occult mindsets (or not even occult, but heretical Christians) the God who commanded Adam and Eve not to eat of the trees is villainous figure and the serpent (who is not Satan and was only identified as Satan later) is a heroic, helpful being that guides man to master himself or deny the illusion of the world and seek past the Demiurge to the true goddess of light and wisdom, Sophia, from whom we have been "stolen" by the self-appointed creator deity, the Abrahamic God. In that case Gendo may mean that original sin has been cleansed through denial. The being that appoints sin is defied and man creates his own moral framework.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Dec 23, 2013 4:37 am I think it's much simpler than that : Gendo is simply referring to who for SEELE the "original sin" refers : for them, mankind and their AT Field separating each other are the source of all their conflicts and misfortune, and thus are "sinners". A "world cleansed of original sin that no man can enter" simply refers to a place that has become completely uninhabitable for mankind(due to the effects of Second Impact), a place that the sinners can't alter and contaminate with their conflicts, and thus "cleansed" of the "original sin"(i.e. of mankind's influence) It's just Gendo's convoluted way to say that the South Pole became a place where mankind can't go anymore, only the perfect beings SEELE want to create with the HIP could live in this "cleansed" place, but he just loves going all cryptic where he's alone with Fuyutsuki.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Dec 25, 2013 2:28 pm ElMariachi wrote: I think it's much simpler than that : Gendo is simply referring to what SEELE the "original sin" : for SEELE, mankind and their AT Field separating each other are the source of all their conflicts and misfortune, and thus are "sinners". A "world cleansed of original sin that no man can enter" simply refers to a place that has become completely uninhabitable for mankind(due to the effects of Second Impact), a place that the sinners can't alter and contaminate with their conflicts, and thus "cleansed" of the "original sin"(i.e. of mankind's influence) It's just Gendo's convoluted way to say that the South Pole became a place where mankind can't go anymore, only the perfect being SEELE want to create with the HIP, he loves going all cryptic where he's alone with Fuyutsuki.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Dec 25, 2013 2:46 pm ^ Oh, well okay. Now as for the Fruits, if Rebuild's lore is the same as NGE about this point, Lilith didn't acquired a FoK, she was created with it, the same way ADAM was created with the FoL. Therefore, Lilith's offspring(the Lilin) all have a FoK, which is what let them be different from one each other, learn new things, be conscious... while ADAM's offspring(the Angels) all have a FoL, which grant them immortality, regeneration... but no consciousness.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Dec 25, 2013 3:57 pm ElMariachi wrote: ^ Oh, well okay. Now as for the Fruits, if Rebuild's lore is the same as NGE about this point, Lilith didn't acquired a FoK, she was created with it

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Dec 25, 2013 5:09 pm unitM wrote: Which makes sense, considering we're all descended from Eve, someone who was forced out from the Garden of Eden, which naturally exposes us to sin.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Sat Dec 28, 2013 3:06 pm ElMariachi wrote: the series and new movies goes to great lengths to show us that they are wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 28, 2013 3:53 pm ^ Shinji rejecting Instrumentality because even though people being separated by the AT Field brings pain, it's also the only way to reach true happiness. That was the whole point of EoE and the series as a whole. What SEELE and Gendo call "sins" is what makes mankind so exceptional and life worth living : our individuality with brought us virtually infinites opportunities of different experiences by meeting and interacting with different people, and the difficulties to connect and understand some people is what makes the successful connections so important and beautiful. In short they only saw the negatives of the Fruit of Knowledge, refusing to acknowledge the positives.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Sat Dec 28, 2013 4:56 pm What you're pointing out doesn't make me wrong though. Yes, sin can be the differences in people. Yes, SEELE's explicit plan is to further humanity by removing those boundaries. But neither of those are indications of whether SEELE's scriptures or their theories surrounding them are right or wrong. Shinji's rejection of instrumentality does not suggest SEELE's plans are wrong either. In fact, Gendou even mentions in the Rebuilds that there is an addition to EoE's scriptures. It is very possible, and in the case of Evangelion, very probable that information is being left out. However, regardless of whether Shinji rejected or did not reject instrumentality bears no weight on whether or not SEELE is "wrong." As for instrumentality, instrumentality is not happiness. Instrumentality is bliss. One can bring up the argument and ask why Shinji didn't experience bliss, and I will answer and say that he did. Perhaps Shinji's form of bliss is discomfort because, judging from his upbringing, it's all he knows. It's feasible that his comfort zone is discomfort. Misery loves company, after all. Perhaps the bliss that Shinji desired was the pain and confusion that affirmed his own beliefs and confidence. Or, alternatively, and also as an extension of this thought, Shinji did not have a goal in mind for instrumentality and thus could not find his bliss. But perhaps that was his bliss - confusion(see above paragraph). Ultimately though, Shinji's rejection of his bliss does not deny SEELE's scripts. Personally, I find that the goal of Instrumentality to be useful and efficient. Did SEELE refuse to acknowledge the positives of mankind? Perhaps. But I can tell you the positives of being in an instrumentality state far outweigh the efficiency of mankind in it's current state. I don't agree that SEELE should have gone about initiating instrumentality on their own means but that doesn't go to say that the end result wasn't a good idea. People are opposed to change, especially change that would turn them into alien goop. The pain of mankind is what makes us beautiful, because beauty springs from chaos and the cleavages of contrasting events, but that doesn't necessarily mean it's good. Living in a blissful state is much more efficient.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Dec 28, 2013 6:38 pm unitM wrote: Personally, I find that the goal of Instrumentality to be useful and efficient. [...] Living in a blissful state is much more efficient.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 28, 2013 7:02 pm unitM wrote: People are opposed to change, especially change that would turn them into alien goop. The pain of mankind is what makes us beautiful, because beauty springs from chaos and the cleavages of contrasting events, but that doesn't necessarily mean it's good. Living in a blissful state is much more efficient.

Yes, SEELE's explicit plan is to further humanity by removing those boundaries. But neither of those are indications of whether SEELE's scriptures or their theories surrounding them are right or wrong. Article: A world cleansed of original sin and the Fruits
 even mentions in the Rebuilds that there is an addition to EoE's scriptures. It is very possible, and in the case of Evangelion, very probable that information is being left out. However, regardless of whether Shinji rejected or did not reject instrumentality bears no weight on whether or not SEELE is "wrong." As for instrumentality, instrumentality is not happiness. Instrumentality is bliss. One can bring up the argument and ask why Shinji didn't experience bliss, and I will answer and say that he did. Perhaps Shinji's form of bliss is discomfort because, judging from his upbringing, it's all he knows. It's feasible that his comfort zone is discomfort. Misery loves company, after all. Perhaps the bliss that Shinji desired was the pain and confusion that affirmed his own beliefs and confidence. Or, alternatively, and also as an extension of this thought, Shinji did not have a goal in mind for instrumentality and thus could not find his bliss. But perhaps that was his bliss - confusion(see above paragraph). Ultimately though, Shinji's rejection of his bliss does not deny SEELE's scripts. Personally, I find that the goal of Instrumentality to be useful and efficient. Did SEELE refuse to acknowledge the positives of mankind? Perhaps. But I can tell you the positives of being in an instrumentality state far outweigh the efficiency of mankind in it's current state. I don't agree that SEELE should have gone about initiating instrumentality on their own means but that doesn't go to say that the end result wasn't a good idea. People are opposed to change, especially change that would turn them into alien goop. The pain of mankind is what makes us beautiful, because beauty springs from chaos and the cleavages of contrasting events, but that doesn't necessarily mean it's good. Living in a blissful state is much more efficient.
